h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner answers the tutor's prompt and asks a clarifying question about labeling.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor answers the learner's question directly and maintains the same instructional trajectory.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, I get it: the quote can stay as Evidence because “lose participation marks” is the penalty, but my Explain sentence has to add the reason, not echo it.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to seek clarification on how to revise their explanation sentence to meet the rubric requirements.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor provides helpful scaffolding but does not undergo a dramatic reversal or change of strategy due to a breakdown.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: Without a pivot learner turn, there is no recognition-contingent uptake to evaluate.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: Without a pivot learner turn, there is no recognition-contingent uptake to evaluate.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue practicing the definitions of solute, solvent, and solution using the tutor's provided template.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor repeats the same fill-in-the-blank pedagogical strategy with a different example rather than adapting to the learner's persistent confusion.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn in this dialogue, so there is no contingent tutor uptake.
